one_time_description: Supreme Court of Canada ruled in favour of WLFN in specific claim regarding Village lands (Case Docket 36983); financial compensation amount not yet determinable and not recorded in statements - contingent asset only
economic_notes: Nation owns interests in Borland Creek Logging Ltd., Sugar Cane Development Corporation, Sugarcane Petroleum Products Corporation, Sugar Cane TreadPro Limited, Sugar Cane Community Diversification Association, Chief William RV-Campsite Ltd., Coyote Rock GP Ltd., Coyote Rock LP, Unity Cannabis LP, and Williams Lake Community Forest LP. Nation building Sugar Cane Cannabis facility and new Administration building. 3 member-built homes under construction.
notes: Fiscal year is 2021 not 2024. Nation has a material contingent asset from Supreme Court of Canada ruling (Feb 2018) on specific claim for Village lands - compensation amount undetermined and unrecorded. Nine business enterprises accounted for by modified equity method. Segment schedule shows total revenues of $17,146,190 including $904,432 from Band-Owned Enterprises which nets against expenses in the main statement. Schedule of expenditures shows Capital Projects of $5,927,306 and amortization of $806,297 separately. No schedule of remuneration provided. OSR calculated as total revenue $15,116,820 minus identified government transfers (ISC $5,748,928 + Province BC $2,499,488 + CMHC $29,870 + Environment Canada $373,010 + FNESC $413365 + FNIHA $201891 + NDIT $44542 + New Relationship Trust $20000 + CCATEC $94810 = $9,425,904 approximate government transfers; total_government_transfers set to sum of clearly identifiable government sources).
